programme.title: rage
programme.desc: Keep the party vibe going as the sun comes up with continuous music videos, from new releases to classics and the songs we've long forgotten.

programme.title: rage
programme.desc: Keep the party vibe going with wall-to-wall music videos, from new releases to classics and the long-forgotten.

programme.title: Weekend Breakfast
programme.desc: All the news you need to start your day. Fauziah Ibrahim and Johanna Nicholson bring you the latest news, interviews, politics, culture, weather and global affairs. Plus sport with Daniela Intili.

programme.title: Insiders
programme.desc: David Speers and the panel unpack the latest in federal politics and national affairs ahead of Anzac Day, the Farrer by-election on Saturday 9 May and preparations for the Federal Budget to be delivered on Tuesday 12 May.

programme.title: Offsiders
programme.desc: More than just the action on the field, Offsiders tackles the week in sport from all angles - from business, politics, marketing, the fans, society, culture, history and the athletes' perspective. Presented by Abbey Gelmi.

programme.title: The World This Week
programme.desc: Girish Sawlani brings you a weekly roundup of international news from an Australian perspective as reported by ABC News correspondents covering the Indo Pacific, Europe, North America, the Middle East, India and Indonesia.

programme.title: Compass
programme.desc: Filmmaker Patrick Abboud meets Palestinian journalists who risked everything to document Gaza. Now safe in Australia, they reveal extraordinary evidence of continued killing of journalists.

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Claire McCollum meets Christians putting faith into action to care for the environment in different ways and discovers cutting-edge technology at a Salvation Army recycling centre.

programme.title: ABC News at Noon
programme.desc: Celina Edmonds presents the latest from ABC News on the weekend bringing you today's top stories and coverage of events as they unfold. Plus original reporting, analysis and updated news, sport, weather and information.

programme.title: Landline
programme.desc: Hosted by award-winning journalist Pip Courtney, Landline is Australia's only national agricultural television program covering stories from Australia's rural and regional heartland.

programme.title: Gardening Australia
programme.sub-title: Autumn: Grow Cacti, Lychees & Organic Veg
programme.desc: Costa explores a vibrant autumn garden, Guest Presenter Thanh gets stuck into lychees, Sophie has natives for formal styles, Jerry finds a huge cacti collection, and we meet radio presenter and veggie gardener Phil Dudman.

programme.title: The Piano
programme.desc: At Melbourne's Dandenong Market, people from all walks of life take a seat at the piano, sharing their stories and musical passion while master musicians Guy Sebastian and Andrea Lam observe.

programme.title: Judgment: Cases That Changed Australia
programme.desc: Two men walk into Hobart police station, confess to a crime and begin High Court battles for justice, respect and marriage equality.

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Howard and Sarah build a sleek metallic floating home on a tidal estuary near Worthing. The structure sits on a polystyrene slab to deal with high tides.

programme.title: Back Roads
programme.desc: Guest Presenter Jeremy Fernandez joins the Sydney Youth Orchestras on an unforgettable musical journey to outback Wilcannia, where they'll perform with local singers, musicians and form new friendships.

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould investigate a landscape painting purported to be by Jean-Baptiste-Camille Corot, a forefather of Impressionism and important 19th century artist.

programme.title: Compass
programme.desc: In her brief residency in East Sydney, the statue of mythical sex worker Joy was celebrated and attacked. Three decades later, supporters mission to have Joy cast in bronze and reinstated.

programme.title: ABC News
programme.desc: News from where we live. ABC News NSW brings you the day's top stories, exclusive investigations and original reporting on the issues that matter to you. Presented by Jeremy Fernandez, plus sport with Jared Coote.

programme.title: The Piano
programme.desc: It's concert time for the five chosen pianists, where they'll be headlining an extraordinary concert at the prestigious City Recital Hall in Sydney.

programme.title: Can You Keep A Secret?
programme.desc: Not only was the doctor a bit hasty in pronouncing Pamela's death - but Neha discovers that Pamela's heart attack wasn't Debbie's fault either. She was being poisoned! Maybe she isn't the blackmailer after all?

programme.title: Frauds
programme.desc: The day of the heist. With tensions high, bitter resentment and revenge in the air, can Sam and Bert keep it together long enough to pull it off and escape each other forever?

programme.title: Blue Murder Motel
programme.desc: Mowai Bay is in the path of a ferocious summer storm, forcing stranded holidaymakers to bunk down in the Blue Motel conference room. Vinny and Cole investigate the death of a local radio DJ.

programme.title: Top End Bub
programme.desc: With Ned living at Leroy's, Lauren and Ned have never felt more distant, and Taya struggles with life without Ned. Trevor gets a makeover and Daffy unveils her song.

programme.title: Two Hands
programme.desc: Jimmy is offered the shot of a lifetime working for local crime boss, Pando. But when he loses a package of $10,000, he finds himself in a whole lot of trouble with the boss and in desperate need of help.

programme.title: rage
programme.desc: Australia's longest-running music show is home to the biggest vault of music videos from a diverse range of artists, genres, and eras. They curate the best clips of all-time to entertain for hours.

programme.title: Gardening Australia
programme.sub-title: Garlic Guide, Wellbeing Garden & Florist Pot
programme.desc: Costa Georgiadis meets students planting bee-friendly highways; Jane Edmanson learns how to create a healing garden space; Millie Ross shows how to grow great garlic; Jerry Coleby-Williams meets a living legend.

programme.title: Dream Gardens
programme.desc: A family revive their tired old garden to complement their new country-style home, but they must mitigate the risk of bushfires to create a garden that is both safe and beautiful.

programme.title: A Bite to Eat with Alice
programme.desc: Singing, dancing and acting cabaret queen Rhonda Burchmore twirls into the kitchen. Alice discovers Rhonda's other passion is fishing. So together they cook up a whole founder - a meal fit for a dancing queen.

programme.title: Insiders
programme.desc: David Speers and the panel cover the latest in politics, national and global affairs including the fuel crisis, supply chain issues, the War in the Middle East, preparations for the Federal Budget and the Farrer by-election.

programme.title: News Breakfast
programme.desc: Start your Monday with all the news you need. James Glenday and Emma Rebellato bring you the latest overnight news, interviews and the top stories this morning, plus Catherine Murphy with sport and Nate Byrne with weather.

programme.title: ABC News Mornings
programme.desc: Stay in the know with what's happening this morning. ABC News brings you up-to-the-minute news to fuel your daily conversations so you can stay connected and follow the discussion across the day. Presented by Gemma Veness.

programme.title: Blue Murder Motel
programme.desc: Mowai Bay is in the path of a ferocious summer storm, forcing stranded holidaymakers to bunk down in the Blue Motel conference room. Vinny and Cole investigate the death of a local radio DJ.

programme.title: Top End Bub
programme.desc: With Ned living at Leroy's, Lauren and Ned have never felt more distant, and Taya struggles with life without Ned. Trevor gets a makeover and Daffy unveils her song.

programme.title: Back Roads
programme.desc: Back Roads visits Cunnamulla in south-west Queensland with guest presenter, 24-year-old Yorta Yorta man and TikTok star Tom Forrest aka 'Outback Tom'.

programme.title: ABC News at Noon
programme.desc: The leading news at lunchtime. Kathryn Robinson brings you today's top stories and the latest news as it unfolds, with reporters in the community across Australia and around the world reporting on the issues that matter.

programme.title: Landline
programme.desc: Hosted by award-winning journalist Pip Courtney, Landline is Australia's only national agricultural television program covering stories from Australia's rural and regional heartland.

programme.title: Tonight At The Museum
programme.desc: An after-hours adventure at the museum as Alex Lee welcomes Pete Helliar, Danielle Walker, Alex Ward and Briggs for laughs and exhibition glory.

programme.title: Back Roads
programme.desc: Guest Presenter Jeremy Fernandez joins the Sydney Youth Orchestras on an unforgettable musical journey to outback Wilcannia, where they'll perform with local singers, musicians and form new friendships.

programme.title: Grand Designs Transformations
programme.desc: In Geelong Victoria, Luke and Bree restore a Victorian cottage and add on a massive contemporary extension, while in the Tasmanian village of Oatlands, Karen and Luke restore a sandstone Georgian cottage.

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: While investigating a murder, Murdoch finds himself teamed with a North West Mounted Policeman whose methods and mannerisms are strikingly similar to his own.

programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next
programme.desc: Davina and Nicky revisit Maureen, who discovered a brother and sister that she previously knew nothing about, and Fiona, whose reunion with her sister brought the revelation that her biological mother was still alive.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team head to Brighton where large crowds have unearthed their family treasures for valuation.

programme.title: Hard Quiz
programme.desc: Four brave contestants step into the Hard Quiz spotlight for a chance to win Tom Gleeson's Big Brass Mug. Tonight's expert topics: anaesthesia, Michael Caine, aircraft manufacturer Boeing, and TV drama Supernatural.

programme.title: ABC News
programme.desc: News from where we live. ABC News NSW brings you the day's top stories, exclusive investigations and original reporting on the issues that matter to you. Presented by Jeremy Fernandez, plus weather with Tom Saunders.

Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Jacob Greber.

programme.title: Australian Story
programme.desc: Two and half years after Belgian backpacker Celine Cremer went missing on a short hike, friends and local volunteers scour the forest and finally find the answers they're looking for.

programme.title: Four Corners
programme.desc: Four Corners returns with powerhouse investigative journalism, exposing scandals, triggering inquiries, leading debate and holding power to account for 65 years.

programme.title: Media Watch
programme.desc: Australia's leading forum for media analysis and comment. Host Linton Besser, a four-time Walkley award-winning investigative reporter and former foreign correspondent, brings you a fearless critique of the week's journalism.

programme.title: Planet America
programme.desc: King Charles is scheduled to visit the US in late April to meet with President Trump, as relations between the US and UK PM Keir Starmer sour over Iran and the Strait of Hormuz. Chas and John guide you through the week.

programme.title: Foreign Correspondent
programme.desc: Fiji is ground zero in the Pacific's drug crisis. Methamphetamine and cocaine are trafficked through Fiji to Australia, fuelling addiction and corruption in the Fijian community.

programme.title: ABC Late News
programme.desc: End your day with the latest on the day's top stories. ABC News has reporters across Australia and around the globe to uncover what's happening and why it matters. Presented by Craig Smart in Perth.

programme.title: The Business
programme.desc: Australia's leading daily business and finance show, bringing you up to date on market moves, economic trends and the forces affecting consumers, employees and business owners big and small. Presented by Kirsten Aiken.

programme.title: The Weekly with Charlie Pickering
programme.desc: Charlie scopes Ben Roberts Smith's jail cell, Rhys Nicholson exposes conspiracies that turned out to be true, Nicolette Minster on hammer-to-the-face beauty fads, and expat comic Felicity Ward sells us on the UK.

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: While investigating a murder, Murdoch finds himself teamed with a North West Mounted Policeman whose methods and mannerisms are strikingly similar to his own.

programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next
programme.desc: Davina and Nicky revisit Maureen, who discovered a brother and sister that she previously knew nothing about, and Fiona, whose reunion with her sister brought the revelation that her biological mother was still alive.

programme.title: rage
programme.desc: Australia's longest-running music show is home to the biggest vault of music videos from a diverse range of artists, genres, and eras. They curate the best clips of all-time to entertain for hours.

programme.title: Gardening Australia
programme.desc: Costa Georgiadis builds a new garden bed; Sophie Thompson meets a Philodendron fanatic; Tino Carnevale demonstrates different ways to support plants; guest presenter Luke Mitchell shows how to care for secateurs.
